title: Address-book loading skeleton has fewer columns than the real table

evidence.quote: // cols=8 must stay in sync with the real address-book table ... <TableSkeleton rows={10} cols={8} />

reasoning: The skeleton explicitly says its column count must match the real address-book table, but it renders 8 columns while the table has 10 headers: Chain, Address, Name, Tags, Notes, Source, Visibility, Created at, Updated at, and Actions. This causes the route-level loading UI to reserve a different table shape than the loaded page, creating avoidable layout shift and stale guidance for future changes.
reproduction: Load `/address-book` slowly enough to see the Next.js `loading.tsx` fallback; the skeleton renders 8 header/cell blocks, then the loaded table swaps to 10 columns.
recommendation: Update `AddressBookLoading` to pass `cols={10}` and refresh the explanatory comment to match the current table headers.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included address-book tests render `page.tsx` and `AddressBookClient`, but none import or assert `loading.tsx`, so the skeleton/table column contract is untested.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a small server-render test for `AddressBookLoading` that counts skeleton header/cell placeholders or snapshots the `TableSkeleton` props against the current table column count.
minimumFixScope: `ui-dashboard/src/app/address-book/loading.tsx` and an optional loading fallback test.

history.note: Fixed AddressBookLoading to render the current 10-column table skeleton and updated loading-state coverage.
